Papaver alpinum, also known as the Alpine Poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the Papaveraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems.

The bioactive constituents of Papaver (genus Papaver) include alkaloids, such as papaverine, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The specific bioactive constituents of Papaver alpinum are not well-documented.
